Based on the detailed anatomical description provided, particularly focusing on the location of the cecum relative to other structures including the ileocecal valve and the fact that the foramen cecum is situated between the perpendicular and cribriform plates of the ethmoid bone, there seems little doubt that the correct identification should remain as option A - Ethmoid bone. All evidence supports this singular location without contradiction. Therefore, despite extensive examination revealing various interesting anatomical features pertinent broadly speaking to intestinal physiology and surgical techniques involving these areas, none alter or cast doubt on the primary assertion regarding the specific location of the foramen cecum.
